scope: Defines terminal pricing hierarchy, last-tradable-close approval policy, unresolved exit policy, and hard-blocker rules for portfolio consumption. This contract governs the boundary between execution-path upstream pricing and portfolio-layer fail-fast enforcement.

cash_settlement.description: Applicable only when an explicit cash_settlement_amount exists and its field provenance is auditable (e.g. exchange-delisted cash buyout, merger consideration). The amount must be traceable to a verifiable source field.

last_tradable_close.description: Applicable only when an auditable close price or adjusted-return basis can be sourced from the last tradable day immediately preceding the terminal event effective date. The price must come from a verifiable close or adjusted-return field, not an approximation.

zero_recovery.description: Conservative fallback used only when neither cash_settlement nor last_tradable_close is available. actual_sell_price is set to zero. Must always carry terminal_exit_conservative_flag=true. This layer must never be used as a convenience workaround.

approval_outcomes.approved_repair_candidate: Row may enter repaired_terminal_event_candidate for the terminal_priced_last_tradable_close path, but it remains a hard blocker until the upstream execution path emits complete actual_exit_date, actual_sell_price, and execution_delayed_realized_return.
approval_outcomes.remain_hard_blocker: Row does not meet the approval gate for repaired_terminal_event_candidate and must remain terminal_event_unpriced.

no_terminal_pricing_source.notes: These rows already expose terminal-event evidence directly in execution_path/project_execution_panel, but they still require formal approval before entering repaired_terminal_event_candidate.

terminal_event_bridge_required.notes: Bridge-standardized rows may enter repaired_terminal_event_candidate only if the last-tradable-close evidence case is approved. The bridge marker must remain as an audit breadcrumb.

approval_requirements: declared last_tradable_date is tradable (not suspended and not no_trade)
bars_daily close > 0 exists on declared last_tradable_date
bars_daily adj_factor > 0 exists on declared last_tradable_date
bars_daily volume > 0 exists on declared last_tradable_date
declared last_tradable_date is suspended or no_trade
actual_last_tradable_date is inferred by scanning backward through tradability_daily and bars_daily

prohibited_actions: Treat repaired_terminal_event_candidate as a priced exit

state_definitions.terminal_priced_cash_settlement: Terminal exit has been priced via the cash_settlement layer. actual_exit_date and actual_sell_price are populated from auditable cash settlement data. terminal_exit_conservative_flag is false.
state_definitions.terminal_priced_last_tradable_close: Terminal exit has been priced via the last_tradable_close layer. actual_exit_date and actual_sell_price are populated from auditable last-tradable-day close or adjusted return. terminal_exit_conservative_flag is false.
state_definitions.terminal_priced_zero_recovery: Terminal exit has been priced via the zero_recovery layer. actual_sell_price is zero. terminal_exit_conservative_flag is true. This state must only be entered as an explicit policy decision, not as a default fallback.
state_definitions.terminal_event_unpriced: A terminal event (e.g. delist) is recorded, but no pricing layer in the hierarchy could be applied. This state also includes upstream bridge-standardized post-delist coverage-gap rows whose terminal event is now explicit but still unpriced. actual_exit_date and actual_sell_price remain NULL. This is a hard blocker and must not enter portfolio.
state_definitions.exit_unresolved: No resolved exit signal and no bridgeable terminal event is recorded for this row. The exit path is entirely unresolved. actual_exit_date is NULL. This is a hard blocker and must not enter portfolio.
state_definitions.calendar_insufficient: Calendar data is insufficient to determine the exit date or to verify tradability windows. This is a hard blocker and must not enter portfolio.

terminal_event_unpriced.reason: A terminal event exists but no pricing was applied. Including this row in portfolio would create an unpriced exit and corrupt realized-return calculations.

exit_unresolved.reason: The exit path is entirely unresolved. actual_exit_date is NULL and no terminal event is recorded. This row cannot contribute to valid holdings or metrics.

calendar_insufficient.reason: Calendar data is insufficient to resolve exit timing or tradability. The exit date cannot be determined.

portfolio_consumption_rules.rule: The portfolio layer MUST fail-fast on any row where actual_exit_date IS NULL or actual_sell_price IS NULL or execution_delayed_realized_return IS NULL. It MUST NOT determine terminal pricing. It MUST NOT filter unresolved rows and continue downstream. It MUST NOT treat planned_exit_date as actual_exit_date.

implementation_boundary.build_project_panels_role: passthrough or standardize only; must not invent or approximate prices for terminal exits
implementation_boundary.zero_recovery_constraint: If zero_recovery is introduced, it must be output as a formal policy decision with terminal_exit_conservative_flag=true, not as a silent portfolio workaround
implementation_boundary.future_implementation_note: Downstream formal implementation of terminal pricing should occur in the execution path or project_execution_panel layer, resolving each terminal event against the pricing hierarchy before the row reaches portfolio. Before that pricing stage, post-delist coverage-gap rows may be bridge-standardized into terminal_event_unpriced but must remain hard blockers.

notes: This contract defines policy and constraints only. It does not implement pricing logic or filter rows.
The terminal pricing hierarchy is ordered: cash_settlement > last_tradable_close > zero_recovery. Higher-ranked layers must be exhausted before falling to the next.
shared_source_degraded_flags.terminal_event_daily in run_input_contract declares the shared source as degraded truth for terminal pricing. Project-side data may supplement but must be auditable.
Approval for repaired_terminal_event_candidate is not equivalent to pricing approval in execution path. The row remains terminal_event_unpriced until complete actual exit fields are emitted upstream.
Any future implementation must produce actual_exit_date, actual_sell_price, and execution_delayed_realized_return before the row reaches portfolio.
